body:
| | | Some folks write you emails pretending to be President Trump.

**I won 't do that to you. **

Some folks pretend donations will somehow fund DOGE.

**I prefer to be honest with you.**

This is Steve Scalise. As your House Majority Leader, I work hand and glove with President Trump, and I love what DOGE is doing for our country.

And when I ask you for support, I want to treat you with respect and be transparent about where your money is going.

You are not an ATM. You are a valued member of our MAGA movement. You deserve to be treated that way.

**_[So that's why I 'm dropping into your inbox right now:](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=6fd9c38abe104548936bbad57ae0829d&_e=VmEhnd_XhAmPaAJepEDKUAH9n79l-y3vwUpgz06kqfGWgKb6W383uki6BowqpB3frKY7H2_KWW2hkXr3vvTGiXSU52qinc6vwkm9xgFCJi-Z7k2iOXQzbEWaIK1iK_4kkm6AAnGGr3ToeotM9PHnGOQYwodkHPptqBzgju3IJqQVJoMpjtLMKMOK7zS-SueH0AumI2ClyJ6XPTu_I9UcrcXL-uQ9f4jfnAwWNOsVzRNgHA1qv7NWn-STVAmwJ4fATj77TH4U44VSKlUY-rhNcGyEgdoYmOGseUO0lgVqPctEhq6MVpt31zY0-L297I34In01GQ7OvtXSoxKaLkSy4VGskIMq36RkUkH3gWhLDT6ymNnFkLVPRSuxCeI8rMg3Aj9WSftWNu9K-0AXontTXA%3D%3D)_**

The Democrat Machine is firing on all cylinders to undermine, stonewall, and sabotage everything President Trump and DOGE are doing to make our government more efficient and cut waste.

Of course, the DC Swamp can't have that.

That's why they're mobilizing the courts, Deep State operatives, and Congressional Democrats to unleash a coordinated political hatchet job against the Trump Administration.

_Democrats are suing him. They 're pushing for partisan investigations. They're firing off hit piece after hit piece in the press. They're introducing legislation to block DOGE's access._

Their plan is simple:
**1.** Attack the Trump Administration
**2.** Drag them into court battles
**3.** Slow them down at every turn
**4.** Use the press to attack their work
**5.** Unload millions to win the Midterms
**6.** Block Trump's ENTIRE legislative agenda
**That 's where House Republicans - and most importantly, YOU - can make a major difference. **

Democrats have already pledged 50 million dollars to win the Midterm elections, take control of Congress, and exploit that power to sabotage EVERYTHING President Trump is doing to save the country. That includes the critical work of DOGE.

Cook Political Report just highlighted EIGHT toss-up districts that will determine who wins the House. If Democrats win even a few seats, it's over. They win the House.

That's why I'm tapping our entire grassroots movement for help.

_**[If you can afford to do so, I 'm imploring you to pitch in a few bucks to save our House Majority - and President Trump's entire legislative agenda along with it.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=6fd9c38abe104548936bbad57ae0829d&_e=VmEhnd_XhAmPaAJepEDKUAH9n79l-y3vwUpgz06kqfGWgKb6W383uki6BowqpB3frKY7H2_KWW2hkXr3vvTGiXSU52qinc6vwkm9xgFCJi-Z7k2iOXQzbEWaIK1iK_4kkm6AAnGGr3ToeotM9PHnGOQYwodkHPptqBzgju3IJqQVJoMpjtLMKMOK7zS-SueH0AumI2ClyJ6XPTu_I9UcrcXL-uQ9f4jfnAwWNOsVzRNgHA1qv7NWn-STVAmwJ4fAKV1_iLYUUeHrcRWinqxAWkS_EaAYq1JfOR3YpdLxrq2sqj97rVDU5kUNmoKPm4-IFJxbdFrinMv_5BdFJGbNyW9pM4obB983q2qFJptxMiN8BQKxGPNNJLBZQNbUlf9xcrMXQZ6HZjTT1wyYuKFtZQ%3D%3D)**___

name: Barack Obama
subject: I’d love to meet you...
body:
I’m excited to share that later this summer, I’ll get to meet a supporter like you and thank you personally for the work you’re doing.
**[You can use this link to make a contribution to the Democratic National Committee and to enter the contest.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-obama-contest?refcode=em250607}&amount=10&refcode2=644_2563979_x79bZq&akid=644%2E2563979%2Ex79bZq)**
Right now, with everything going on, it would be easy to give in to fear or despair — but people like you aren’t doing that. You’re showing up at town halls in your communities, holding your representatives accountable, and maybe even planning to run for office yourself.
I’m excited to say thank you in person.
**[If you use this email to chip in $10 or any amount that feels right to you to support the DNC’s work, you’ll be automatically entered to meet with me. Plus, you’ll get to bring a guest, too.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-obama-contest?refcode=em250607}&amount=10&refcode2=644_2563979_x79bZq&akid=644%2E2563979%2Ex79bZq)**

Friend,
---
Before you dread the idea of reading another political email, I want you to know this one’s different.
---
No doom and gloom, no panic. What I’m about to share with you is PROOF that when we step up and answer the call, we BEAT the Left and WIN.
---
When I last reached out, I unfortunately had troubling news to share...
---
**_At the time, polling from Fox News showed Democrats with a _7-point lead_ on the generic congressional ballot._**
---
But just one month later, after following up with countless Republican voters to verify their voter profiles to give me and President Trump the _TRUTH_ about where voters stand in 2025, I couldn’t be more pleased with the results.
---
**_In fact, Rasmussen just released a poll showing the Democrats’ lead has been CUT DOWN to just 1 POINT... well within the _margin_ _of_ _error_!_**
---
We are closing the gap to not only defend but EXPAND President Trump’s majority – and the Left is terrified.
---
Friend, we’ve crunched the numbers and with our razor-thin majority now within the margin of error, YOUR voter profile is the difference maker.
---
**That’s why I’m reaching out today to simply ask that you VERIFY your GOP voter profile to give me and President Trump the most accurate polling possible as we CLOSE THE GAP on the Democrats.**

---
[VERIFY NOW ](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8j82SmzAQhJ9muJkSIyHBgUMcwh6cv0oO2ZxcMhpsbSHAkjC1-_QpKOKjuj9Nd2s39bZ7r0AdAfFmTQBE4J8A8f53cX5-c6eXw8vy8Xq4y-8uX138vKMPfMJDq4eP4-_TpY5Z-PKqgrneT4_sicf3iZ7wHpqS07ZfVVUnpuKdYSKhKlOi5BnnQiS3imuGZafJdKxAgZeioE6QzEkWZYulTGyFDHMmmcokImJadqhYxy9MFybLuxIE23JS7cjbVmchLtr7cXRpO7qkr24xTmEr1gA2gdrZU7rYwZNZCcAmknaHt9EbPQA2hSmEKdVFFUopIfn6jzdzdOdWu0nb6wC83kvlgHJ1HBk7O-D1PnkTwzj7loDXv_6cj7vWjkOkIQKvf3z99hNQajfO2xv_34rk3ZYgc3lefJb4ykyL7XsbpALBrtvaddujwn8BAAD__3qEkbc)

Thank you,

Jim Jordan

name: Former Navy SEAL Adam Schwarze
subject: I’m running against Tim Walz’s radical lieutenant …
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
I’m just an ordinary guy from Minnesota who was asked to do extraordinary things for our country now I am asking you to open this and read the first few paragraphs | [](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=34368f7bd86a486ba3bac9d459a41dc1&_e=dGrOff389UDZAqq6W4x7yfCerusX01mgNxcu8ajZpuSdEh06Vr3hks3cCF41kmZjsYXFsiezRDAOfGBSs2fSXXX4VbxRD8tvBfK6XGEsISAx7TTzUD_dR-8tztlTwqQY-dRCN2vje9eqemiTf09vHXFp_aoDdIfxkRY-HZx4UoLao9KNX1iYFtuOnpbVJgaXM99aU_botEB1ZYlPMVFx7FuIO8JCUqIu60D0GNUqy4l4ySJeHDkM6uQz_yuOqcF_pTgF4CCDPMQlPiTsswIZYpZpqN_eY0Cc8s0jzOHShvoOZ5S6qUHStgEf9ZwfmdQwg323Zm-Y5oTA7vNop8guxNqbvNBc-yjRlOZ5COEngrWh3p4WjU4OtTMQC28ecSIy3Ec1xx2IftG8RqVV934VITtvZaiqnwVsFMFNSNgkA34%3D)

---

Fellow Conservative,

My name is Adam Schwarze, and _**I’m a former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps vet, avid hockey player, and lifelong Minnesotan.**_ I know it’s early in the 2026 election cycle and you may not be paying attention to races in their infancy, like mine, still, I’m reaching out to you today to tell you a little about myself, who I am, and to make my case to earn your support in the upcoming battle I’m currently running headfirst into.

I’m running for the U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor, who, if you can believe it, **is politically more radical than the former VP nominee**.
**_
_**_People like the lieutenant governor were leading the violent unrest in Minneapolis in the summer of 2020._**_While the governor, whom the nation embarrassingly became acquainted with, eventually called for law and order in the street; his lieutenant governor was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police Department for discrimination._**

The Minnesota I grew up in was far away from the world these two government officials call home.

[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=34368f7bd86a486ba3bac9d459a41dc1&_e=dGrOff389UDZAqq6W4x7yfCerusX01mgNxcu8ajZpuSdEh06Vr3hks3cCF41kmZjsYXFsiezRDAOfGBSs2fSXXX4VbxRD8tvBfK6XGEsISAx7TTzUD_dR-8tztlTwqQY-dRCN2vje9eqemiTf09vHXFp_aoDdIfxkRY-HZx4UoLao9KNX1iYFtuOnpbVJgaXM99aU_botEB1ZYlPMVFx7FuIO8JCUqIu60D0GNUqy4l4ySJeHDkM6uQz_yuOqcF_pTgF4CCDPMQlPiTsswIZYh9EWDOgbG0taxu2aZRLio_UuFQb71ecHoVcEJE9VuOCS0s_8enojK8le3UMxjP8fUNhzXWFF2TI82lKtLa62n56x0DUzi01hdpu06FernuiPC3Ilah6AfQ8GGKPCIT4_wH9YYUdFzbhYb5TYw3e_vM%3D)

As the son of two working-class parents, I have nothing in common with either my opponent or her boss, former VP candidate and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz.

I grew up playing hockey and joined the military on my 18th birthday in October 2001, less than two months after the horrific attack on New York, Pennsylvania, and Washington, D.C. Unlike Governor Walz, I was proud to deploy to over 70 countries in service to our nation;**I didn’t retire before being shipped out to serve our nation in war. I’ve willingly put myself into harm’s way more times than I can count in order to protect the American people, our shared conservative values, and our democratic way of life.**

I never imagined that I would run for high office, but _**everytime I came home from deployment things in Minnesota and the country seemed to have gotten worse because of the radical left policies.**_ I’m doing this because of the state of our current leadership. Minnesota is currently being led by two radical Democrats who have done their best to push this state as far left as anyone could tolerate.

**Something had to be done. **

With Sen. Tina Smith’s recent retirement announcement, now is a time to leap into the unknown—something I’ve done and found very fulfilling. While Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential election since 1976, we’re not a deep blue state.

This election isn’t going to be an easy one for a Republican to win, but throughout my life and career I have always defied the odds. _**And as a former Navy SEAL and Marine Corps veteran, I excel in harsh environments - on and off the battlefield.**_

**[You don’t know me, and you have no real reason to make a financial contribution to my Senate campaign other than that I’m humbly asking you to do so.](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=34368f7bd86a486ba3bac9d459a41dc1&_e=dGrOff389UDZAqq6W4x7yfCerusX01mgNxcu8ajZpuSdEh06Vr3hks3cCF41kmZjsYXFsiezRDAOfGBSs2fSXXX4VbxRD8tvBfK6XGEsISAx7TTzUD_dR-8tztlTwqQY-dRCN2vje9eqemiTf09vHXFp_aoDdIfxkRY-HZx4UoLao9KNX1iYFtuOnpbVJgaXM99aU_botEB1ZYlPMVFx7FuIO8JCUqIu60D0GNUqy4l4ySJeHDkM6uQz_yuOqcF_pTgF4CCDPMQlPiTsswIZYqxBJUKWytufkCZY8YO-4vWm1wOQt-FVrI9_znK712oidpiR9IQokdeAefBWHpN0LvB06u0DVLhtzb0jnvH4YG15XBk2b_9671JH13K6DFF0Lw0CAy3GxpJwHu5RiwSRG2ooRREBLnoU2SWGTFXW3hY%3D)**

[**HELP ME GET MY CAMPAIGN OFF THE GROUND WITH A GIFT OF $5 OR MORE**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=34368f7bd86a486ba3bac9d459a41dc1&_e=dGrOff389UDZAqq6W4x7yfCerusX01mgNxcu8ajZpuSdEh06Vr3hks3cCF41kmZjsYXFsiezRDAOfGBSs2fSXXX4VbxRD8tvBfK6XGEsISAx7TTzUD_dR-8tztlTwqQY-dRCN2vje9eqemiTf09vHXFp_aoDdIfxkRY-HZx4UoLao9KNX1iYFtuOnpbVJgaXM99aU_botEB1ZYlPMVFx7FuIO8JCUqIu60D0GNUqy4l4ySJeHDkM6uQz_yuOqcF_pTgF4CCDPMQlPiTsswIZYsEyha8vKdMsFaN9M8PAjk1lGYtGlZpMIEXzonTBgQL9syzmeACbnU4CeVOYg07JNjORbExSkgIEf2o0Rak4UJh8yx6wScrPEmtFDWr2X4jbbuYa4K0aryz2r-AU_QAF7v1uob6XQ7uJHeUaHxvIAa4%3D)
_**I love our country, so on my 18th birthday in October 2001, I enlisted in the United States Marine Corps with a deep desire to serve my nation, make my parents proud, and defend freedoms.**_

Twenty-four years later, I’m still looking to serve Minnesotans in service to our nation - that's why I'm running for the United States Senate.

[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=34368f7bd86a486ba3bac9d459a41dc1&_e=dGrOff389UDZAqq6W4x7yfCerusX01mgNxcu8ajZpuSdEh06Vr3hks3cCF41kmZjsYXFsiezRDAOfGBSs2fSXXX4VbxRD8tvBfK6XGEsISAx7TTzUD_dR-8tztlTwqQY-dRCN2vje9eqemiTf09vHXFp_aoDdIfxkRY-HZx4UoLao9KNX1iYFtuOnpbVJgaXM99aU_botEB1ZYlPMVFx7FuIO8JCUqIu60D0GNUqy4l4ySJeHDkM6uQz_yuOqcF_pTgF4CCDPMQlPiTsswIZYletQ5f9njlZlfRZ4fHvhTMxgr2NNLaocgzzjESa4OjZ2rtXco5B-jZLVisMINTnSwUidYWYbUgxDYrt_izT4F5ireMHDPWggWFMrXh4IGq5vEUE8KDB-Y3BZIYlRvSBL-gcL_Or5y_rp9p0og-9pjc%3D)

Since 2001, I’ve been deployed nine times to 70 countries to defend our shared conservative American values.

I obtained a Master's degree from Harvard and a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins University in Security and Nuclear Deterrence. I also received my MBA degree.

[**CHIP IN TO SEND A NAVY SEAL TO THE SENATE**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=34368f7bd86a486ba3bac9d459a41dc1&_e=dGrOff389UDZAqq6W4x7yfCerusX01mgNxcu8ajZpuSdEh06Vr3hks3cCF41kmZjsYXFsiezRDAOfGBSs2fSXXX4VbxRD8tvBfK6XGEsISAx7TTzUD_dR-8tztlTwqQY-dRCN2vje9eqemiTf09vHXFp_aoDdIfxkRY-HZx4UoLao9KNX1iYFtuOnpbVJgaXM99aU_botEB1ZYlPMVFx7FuIO8JCUqIu60D0GNUqy4l4ySJeHDkM6uQz_yuOqcF_pTgF4CCDPMQlPiTsswIZYgbWpjcyw6av555-VHz0QaC4rnwcOBzO-weyYcFgKdhA5V5X-BYwbYe8CQi60HGgx0VchkCT8fs-90gn8_gBqKohCOWhD7zVRKnBKI9wTUQ9ioPQ_30ZMlp8JhcAK6PAZBzeJELIiVFVLKefm0MzWBg%3D)
So when people tell me this will be one of the most challenging things I’ll ever endure, I smile and nod.

clean_body: I’m just an ordinary guy from Minnesota who was asked to do extraordinary things for our country now I am asking you to open this and read the first few paragraphs | Fellow Conservative, My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps vet, avid hockey player, and lifelong Minnesotan. I know it’s early in the 2026 election cycle and you may not be paying attention to races in their infancy, like mine, still, I’m reaching out to you today to tell you a little about myself, who I am, and to make my case to earn your support in the upcoming battle I’m currently running headfirst into. I’m running for the U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor, who, if you can believe it, is politically more radical than the former VP nominee. People like the lieutenant governor were leading the violent unrest in Minneapolis in the summer of 2020.While the governor, whom the nation embarrassingly became acquainted with, eventually called for law and order in the street; his lieutenant governor was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police Department for discrimination. The Minnesota I grew up in was far away from the world these two government officials call home. As the son of two working-class parents, I have nothing in common with either my opponent or her boss, former VP candidate and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz. I grew up playing hockey and joined the military on my 18th birthday in October 2001, less than two months after the horrific attack on New York, Pennsylvania, and Washington, D.C. Unlike Governor Walz, I was proud to deploy to over 70 countries in service to our nation;I didn’t retire before being shipped out to serve our nation in war. I’ve willingly put myself into harm’s way more times than I can count in order to protect the American people, our shared conservative values, and our democratic way of life. I never imagined that I would run for high office, but everytime I came home from deployment things in Minnesota and the country seemed to have gotten worse because of the radical left policies. I’m doing this because of the state of our current leadership. Minnesota is currently being led by two radical Democrats who have done their best to push this state as far left as anyone could tolerate. Something had to be done. With Sen. Tina Smith’s recent retirement announcement, now is a time to leap into the unknown—something I’ve done and found very fulfilling. While Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential election since 1976, we’re not a deep blue state. This election isn’t going to be an easy one for a Republican to win, but throughout my life and career I have always defied the odds. And as a former Navy SEAL and Marine Corps veteran, I excel in harsh environments - on and off the battlefield. I love our country, so on my 18th birthday in October 2001, I enlisted in the United States Marine Corps with a deep desire to serve my nation, make my parents proud, and defend freedoms. Twenty-four years later, I’m still looking to serve Minnesotans in service to our nation - that's why I'm running for the United States Senate. Since 2001, I’ve been deployed nine times to 70 countries to defend our shared conservative American values. I obtained a Master's degree from Harvard and a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins University in Security and Nuclear Deterrence. I also received my MBA degree. So when people tell me this will be one of the most challenging things I’ll ever endure, I smile and nod. The simple truth is that I believe that my purpose in this life is to serve others, and I’m just a regular guy from Minnesota who was asked to do extraordinary things who wants to make my mom proud, serve my country, and do good by the people I meet. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

Rebecca has built a coalition of voters of all stripes because she understands working folks don’t need gossip, they need stability. And that insulting Trump doesn’t undo the very real cuts that are harming Wisconsin families.
**Her priority will always be creating policy that works for us -- not the elite.**
But after her appearance, the nation is again watching WI-03. And they should.
**What happens here has national implications. Not only do we have a great chance to send Derrick Van Orden packing, but winning this race replaces a major ally of the mega-rich and powerful with a working-class champion.**
[**Help us keep our momentum going: Chip in $3 to elect a representative who will fight for what working families need.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109167349/550213994/1132932056?refcode=em-6.7.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYjk5YmY4Ny1iYzQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WHD_F5TIr4sK3z_M_ZyHS30P2UVi2PXCOLqFItp4dvk=)
****[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109167352/550213997/1132932056?refcode=em-6.7.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYjk5YmY4Ny1iYzQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WHD_F5TIr4sK3z_M_ZyHS30P2UVi2PXCOLqFItp4dvk=)****

Rebecca Cooke is a small business and nonprofit leader who has focused her career on building community and serving others. She was born and raised on an Eau Claire dairy farm and was appointed by Governor Tony Evers to the Wisconsin Economic Development Corporation.

Marcy Kaptur is a fighter for high-wage jobs, growing a workforce to meet the needs of the 21st century, and protecting our region’s most vital natural resource, the Great Lakes. She’s a voice for us all in the People’s House.

Here’s more about why I’m in this race:
We outperformed every Democratic race in our district in 2024, reaching more than five points better than the top of the ticket, and even outperforming the winning Senate race.
We did it with a coalition that united working Wisconsinites, ranging from labor unions, to farmers, to students, and even voters who went with Trump.
And since that time, our call to put real, working class Wisconsinites first has only become more critical.
These past couple of months have the American people reeling from crushing attacks from this administration – policies that have put billionaires first and hit working families the hardest.
I’m feeling it personally. I’m still waitressing at the Good Wives in Eau Claire, and I hear the same ongoing concerns from folks in our community. Basic costs of living like groceries and utility costs are still on the rise, health care is less accessible and affordable, family farms are folding, and home ownership feels further and further out of reach.
We can’t sit on the sidelines as the elites take up every last resource and leave all of us behind. We need action to have public service that works for us.
**I know what working families need, because I’m part of one. And I wouldn’t be in this race if I wasn’t confident we could finish the job. But I can’t do this alone:**

name: Katie Porter for Governor
subject: Our June fundraising deadline is different
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
A quick explainer on why this deadline is the most important one yet.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=39786%2E3422442%2EgVTzQJ)
Dear Derek,
We have a _really_ important fundraising deadline coming up at the end of June. Unlike our other monthly milestones so far, this June deadline is public facing, which means we’ll have to report for everyone to see exactly how much money we’ve raised.
**This is a make or break moment for our campaign.**
Pundits, outside groups, and our opponents will all be combing through our fundraising numbers to look for signs of weakness. We have to show them that our grassroots movement is strong, and it’s not going anywhere.
Katie has been leading in the polls because Californians want a leader who will deliver for families and work to bring costs down. But with 10+ declared candidates and a majority of voters undecided, we can’t take anything for granted.
**Posting a strong June fundraising number—especially from grassroots donations—will send a message that our campaign has the momentum it takes to win.**
We reject donations from corporations, lobbyists, and Big Pharma, Big Oil, and Big Bank CEOs, and our average donation is less than $30. We can’t do this without you.
**[Can you chip in $5 today to help us show everyone that Katie is the best person to represent California and fight for working families?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250607_1_ak?refcode=em20250607-39786&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39786_3422442_gVTzQJ&akid=39786%2E3422442%2EgVTzQJ)**
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250607_1_ak?refcode=em20250607-39786&refcode2=39786_3422442_gVTzQJ&akid=39786%2E3422442%2EgVTzQJ)
Thanks for being with us,
Team KP

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20250607-39786&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=39786_3422442_gVTzQJ&akid=39786%2E3422442%2EgVTzQJ)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=39786&t=4&akid=39786%2E3422442%2EgVTzQJ)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=39786)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=39786&t=5&akid=39786%2E3422442%2EgVTzQJ)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
FFPC #1479597

The Left wants to take parents out of the equation and hand over life-altering decisions to schools, judges, and radical activists.**But AMAC Action believes parents—not woke ideologues—should have the final say in what’s best for their children.** This battle has never been more important, because a poll from one of these “pro-trans” organizations claims they’ve found **_“High Support Among Parents for Transgender, Nonbinary Children”..._**
---
_“Most adults would be willing to take all actions tested to support their child if they came out as transgender or nonbinary, including: encouraging other family members or friends to respect their gender identity (71%); use their chosen name and pronouns correctly (69%); and not vote for political candidates that support anti-transgender policies (65%)”_
---
Worse, here are some of the supposed “findings” from these radical pro-trans organizations:
---
* “38,600 transgender youth live in states that have “banned access” to facilities, such as bathrooms”.
* “120,200 transgender youth live in states that restrict access to school athletics.”
* 38% of transgender adolescents live in states with laws or policies restricting their access to gender-affirming care.

---

**My fellow patriot, my name is Rebecca Weber and I’m the Chairwoman of AMAC Action. **** ** “AMAC” stands for Association of Mature American Citizens. As a senior how do you feel about their findings? Are you concerned about “38,600 transgender youth being banned from locker-rooms”?_Or...**Are you more concerned about the hundreds of thousands of “transgender” adults WHO ARE right now free to use the restroom with REAL women and girls? Are you worried about the 120,200 “transgender youth” living in states that “restrict access to school athletics?” Or ...Are you scared that your daughters or granddaughters might be forced to compete with,and be injured by, a biological male? I stand on the side of parental rights and the protection of our children. name: Josh Harder
subject: I’m furious
body:
Who do they think they are?

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109170444/550225280/-1860252395?refcode=-header-&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2FlM2YwNmYtYzY0My1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Vo4q1GB4J6SYRq3s300bkCaXhcXjiPBvBrB-DWCtCl8=) | Peter, I’ll be direct, I’m pretty furious. **This week, national Republicans launched a massive ad blitz targeting Latino voters in my district. They are intentionally spreading misinformation about my record and they’re doing it in Spanish where they think they won’t get caught.** Here’s an example of one of the ads: **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109170445/550225281/1736758198?&refcode=E13561-20250605-LatinoResponse-SB-CampaignNews&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2FlM2YwNmYtYzY0My1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Vo4q1GB4J6SYRq3s300bkCaXhcXjiPBvBrB-DWCtCl8=)** I think it’s pretty despicable for national Republicans to intentionally mislead our Latino community. So, the next question is, of course: What are we going to do about it? I need your help to launch our own Spanish-language response ads and to get our Latino organizing efforts started. These investments are critical in a toss-up district like mine with over 40% of Latino residents! [_**Can I count on you to chip in right now to help us make these urgent, strategic investments? This could _literally_ be the difference between keeping this seat blue or giving Republicans another 2 years in power.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109170446/550225282/1736758198?&refcode=E13561-20250605-LatinoResponse-SL-CampaignNews&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2FlM2YwNmYtYzY0My1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Vo4q1GB4J6SYRq3s300bkCaXhcXjiPBvBrB-DWCtCl8=) I can’t overstate how critical this moment is. We have to act fast. \- Josh

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Killed while waiting for food for their starving families
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

For the third time in three days, Israeli soldiers massacred Palestinians
simply trying to get the only food that Israel has allowed to enter Gaza.
On Tuesday, they killed 27 Palestinians, the day before they killed 3, and
on Sunday they killed at least 31—all for trying to feed their families.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em20250603-signup-gazaaidmassacres?source=em20250607-36742&akid=36742%2E1203867%2EZ2vPcG ]The New York Times: Israeli Soldiers Open Fire Near Gaza Aid Site. Gaza
Officials Say 27 Are Killed. It was the second large-scale shooting by
Israeli forces in three days near a food distribution center, which is
part of a contentious new Israeli and American-backed initiative.

These aid massacres are just the latest violent actions taken by the
Israeli military after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu broke the
ceasefire deal a few months ago, with Donald Trump’s full support.

They are shooting starving people desperately trying to access food,
continuing to drop bombs on tents and schools housing refugees, including
children and infants, and causing a man-made famine that could kill
millions of Palestinians.

Any of our elected leaders who continue to support this deserve to be
challenged in the upcoming primary elections — there is no excuse that
justifies this genocide and ethnic cleansing.

We need an arms embargo NOW. Justice Democrats’ members like Rashida
Tlaib, Delia Ramirez, Ilhan Omar, and Summer Lee are leading the call
against these war crimes, but we need to demand every Democrat stand up
and do what’s in their power to stop this.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em20250603-signup-gazaaidmassacres?source=em20250607-36742&akid=36742%2E1203867%2EZ2vPcG ]Add your name to our petition urging EVERY Democrat in Congress to stop
enabling genocide and support an arms embargo against the Israeli military
ASAP!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em20250603-signup-gazaaidmassacres?source=em20250607-36742&akid=36742%2E1203867%2EZ2vPcG ]Add Your Name

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250607-36742-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36742_1203867_Z2vPcG&akid=36742%2E1203867%2EZ2vPcG

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36742%2E1203867%2EZ2vPcG

name: Chairman Tim Scott
subject: Can I ask you something, Friend?
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj2052pj8aptvj46z7ykdx61ifutqe5vt2cj2pqstgl604dciaztj3mjrm63fovux1lemazsayuhj3ao8v8fy&__stmp=sxhzdj&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxhzdj&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jf13czze4doh3f9giijtwx6p56aie7r8dwx8euo70oh2jrrkss2lrpris3px6azp2hltd3jkltnwcqgwzmd5nn5ghdgs7auyfkmmz6xgacr0t6mp1hp3vdxive2os0b2x596ypefy1ue0je2p7je&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxhzdj&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxhzdj&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxhzdj&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxhzdj&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jf13czze4doh3f9giijtwx6pqfdollp3vteatabg6bsjdc5i6zc5xiiqyytmzm2qkf1fdzpg255i2uwrsrh0jjrxwazr4yfycb55tayt16sue3lw3esc6d868bo9ig8yjqkvqgrft38tuyhpmipy&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxhzdj&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sxhzdj&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: Derek Tran
subject: Something I took personally.
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109171221/550227768/2039042026?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOWIwMWMwNTQtYzk0My1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=BkfyUxsuv7qWdExJwUmQglqTvlsqaRJHcqqj2V3BJIY=)
Hi there, Unlike many in Congress, I’m not a career politician. And while I’m new to the “swamp” in Washington, I know betrayal when I see it. At a recent CNN town hall I joined, one of my Republican colleagues stood in front of the American people and promised he’d _never_ vote to cut Medicaid. **Then he voted to gut it by billions.** I know what it’s like to count on Medicaid. I’m the son of refugees, and it helped my family stay afloat. Without that support, I would likely not be serving in Congress today. So, when someone stands on stage and makes a promise to the millions of people who depend on these programs, then breaks that promise when it’s politically convenient, I take that personally. Time and again, Republicans say one thing and do another. It isn’t right, but without the House majority it’s nearly impossible to hold this extremist machine accountable. **[Look, this is one of the most competitive races in the entire country. I flipped this seat by a handful of votes, and it’s made me a top target. clean_body: | = Hi there, Unlike many in Congress, I’m not a career politician. And while I’m new to the “swamp” in Washington, I know betrayal when I see it. At a recent CNN town hall I joined, one of my Republican colleagues stood in front of the American people and promised he’d never vote to cut Medicaid. Then he voted to gut it by billions. I know what it’s like to count on Medicaid. I’m the son of refugees, and it helped my family stay afloat. Without that support, I would likely not be serving in Congress today. So, when someone stands on stage and makes a promise to the millions of people who depend on these programs, then breaks that promise when it’s politically convenient, I take that personally. Time and again, Republicans say one thing and do another. It isn’t right, but without the House majority it’s nearly impossible to hold this extremist machine accountable. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Let’s hold the line and make sure the folks who talk a big game finally face some accountability. subject: Our farmers deserve so much better than what Trump is offering.

body:
This downright pisses me off. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109171747/550229734/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZmU5N2FlYS1jOTQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vWB27izrV6z0fLjFQxZ5JvfjquYwFEHuqzDseKmfVNs=&emci=8e1cf82e-6f42-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=cfe97aea-c943-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15820338)
---

Today is World Food Safety Day, Peter.

I’m thinking about the hardworking farmers and growers who make food safety possible.

Here in Washington’s 8th District, agriculture isn’t just an industry—it’s a way of life. Our district has a rich and proud legacy of growing apples, cherries, and more. The farms and the farmers who keep them running have helped power our local economy for generations.

But they do more than that, Peter: _They help put food on tables across this country and the world._ That’s why I’m furious that the Trump administration and Congressional Republicans are trying to gut agriculture funding while ignoring the devastating consequences for working Americans. And that’s on top of the tariff wars that have an outsized impact on US agriculture.

The proposed Republican budget would slash nearly $7 billion from the USDA. That includes over $1 billion cut from farm production and conservation programs, as well as over $2 billion stripped from rural development. Programs that support crop insurance, clean energy initiatives, and even basic food safety inspections are all on the chopping block.

Peter, these cuts would hurt Washington’s 8th District. They’d hurt our farmers. They’d hurt our rural communities. And they’d hurt families across the country who rely on our growers to safely and sustainably feed the nation. Frankly, it doesn’t just dishearten me—**it downright pisses me off**. But time and time again, Republicans have shown that they’ll side with corporate interests over working families—even if it means dismantling the very backbone of American agriculture.

**[I’m standing firm in Congress against these reckless proposals. But the truth is, we won’t stop the GOP’s carnage unless we take back the House. That’s where you come in. Chip in $5, or anything you can, to help me power this campaign and flip the House blue in 2026.>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109171748/550229736/-1978405529?refcode=em25_250607_USDA_fndd_gq_act___KS_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em25_250607_USDA_fndd_gq_act___KS_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=cfe97aea-c943-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&refcode2=cfe97aea-c943-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZmU5N2FlYS1jOTQzLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vWB27izrV6z0fLjFQxZ5JvfjquYwFEHuqzDseKmfVNs=)**

clean_body: This downright pisses me off. Today is World Food Safety Day, Peter. I’m thinking about the hardworking farmers and growers who make food safety possible. Here in Washington’s 8th District, agriculture isn’t just an industry—it’s a way of life. Our district has a rich and proud legacy of growing apples, cherries, and more. The farms and the farmers who keep them running have helped power our local economy for generations. But they do more than that, Peter: They help put food on tables across this country and the world. That’s why I’m furious that the Trump administration and Congressional Republicans are trying to gut agriculture funding while ignoring the devastating consequences for working Americans. And that’s on top of the tariff wars that have an outsized impact on US agriculture. The proposed Republican budget would slash nearly $7 billion from the USDA. That includes over $1 billion cut from farm production and conservation programs, as well as over $2 billion stripped from rural development. Programs that support crop insurance, clean energy initiatives, and even basic food safety inspections are all on the chopping block. Peter, these cuts would hurt Washington’s 8th District. They’d hurt our farmers. They’d hurt our rural communities. And they’d hurt families across the country who rely on our growers to safely and sustainably feed the nation. Frankly, it doesn’t just dishearten me—it downright pisses me off. But time and time again, Republicans have shown that they’ll side with corporate interests over working families—even if it means dismantling the very backbone of American agriculture. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Onward, Kim Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. It’s easy to feel hopeless in moments like this. But that is exactly what our opponents want. They flood the zone with chaos, again and again, until people throw their hands up and give up. **But we refuse to walk away from this fight.** At JanePAC, we’re building something stronger than Trump’s chaos: a nationwide firewall of elected climate champions. clean_body: We can’t afford to look away. | We wanted to make sure you saw these headlines, because we can’t stop thinking about them: = Every day that we turn on the news, we see a new attack on climate progress or another reckless decision that puts our lives at risk. It’s easy to feel hopeless in moments like this. But that is exactly what our opponents want. They flood the zone with chaos, again and again, until people throw their hands up and give up. But we refuse to walk away from this fight. At JanePAC, we’re building something stronger than Trump’s chaos: a nationwide firewall of elected climate champions. We’re supporting brave climate leaders in races that traditionally go overlooked and underfunded — from the state legislature down to our local school boards. But here’s the truth: Our work can only succeed with support from people like you digging a bit deeper in make-or-break moments like this. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Let’s do this! Team JanePAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

Dear Peter,
---
What was once a dream team is now turning into a nightmare. The Trump/Musk feud is driving a wedge into the amazing work that was helping to restore our nation. I have friends in the Trump camp and friends in the Musk camp, and after talking with all sides, it’s clear that this is spiritual warfare. I have a lot more thoughts on the specifics—watch the video below to hear my full take on the Trump/Musk feud, and be praying for a resolution to the spirits of division and accusation that are plaguing our leaders.

Peter, I am endlessly grateful for this team’s support. Every time someone chips in a grassroots donation, it’s a strong reminder that there’s a team of people like you who believe in my work. Like Gordon in Saxtons River, who chipped in $25. Or Becky in Rutland, who pitched in $10. Even all the way in Glendale, Arizona — Byron pitched in a few bucks, too. To tell you the truth, **it doesn’t take much to create an impact on this people-powered movement when I can count on supporters like you. ** I'm fighting for an economy that works for working people, not the billionaire class. When you chip in to my campaign, it shows that there's a movement of people behind me who believe in my vision for a fairer future. _And it shows we don’t need super PACs or special interests to get it done. _ I’m in this for people like Becky, Gordon, and you, Peter. That’s who I’m fighting for in Congress, and it’s why we’re laying the foundation to retake a House majority that will stand with everyday people — not billionaires. **[I’d be honored to earn your support today. Will you chip in your first donation to my campaign? **Take a look at these names:** These 21 Republican Senators are doing Trump’s bidding in the Senate. Tommy Tuberville. Dan Sullivan. Tom Cotton. Ashley Moody. Jim Risch. Joni Ernst. Roger Marshall. Bill Cassidy. Susan Collins. Cindy Hyde-Smith. Steve Daines. Pete Ricketts. Thom Tillis. Jon Husted. Markwayne Mullin. Lindsey Graham. Mike Rounds. Bill Hagerty. John Cornyn. Shelley Moore Capito. Cynthia Lummis. These Senators don’t care a lick about doing what’s right for the American people. Right now, they’re gearing up to cut health insurance for _millions_ of Americans and put families who rely on SNAP at risk — all to give the ultra-wealthy a fat tax cut. **If we can hold on to seats currently held by Democrats in states like Georgia and Michigan, and flip just FOUR (!) of these GOP-held Senate seats, we can Flip the Senate and hold Trump accountable for his dangerous agenda.** But we know Donald Trump and his allies are going to spend BIG to defend these seats and expand their majority. We were already a top target for the GOP, and this all but guarantees Republicans will be spending tens of millions of dollars to try and buy this seat.

**Here’s the good news:** A recent poll showed me as the only Democrat likely to defeat Trump’s handpicked candidate Mike Rogers – the same Republican who benefited from the GOP’s $80 million war chest last year.

Make no mistake, this race will be extremely close, and the Senate majority will likely run through Michigan. That’s why we can’t afford to sit back.

I’ve been a fighter my entire life. In 2018, I flipped a red district blue and helped take back the House. And now? I’m ready to keep standing up for every Michigander concerned about rising costs, erratic tariffs, and cuts to programs that working families have paid into their entire lives.

body:
[1]Powered By People

Hi,

We don’t stop organizing just because it’s not an election year.

In fact, it’s during these moments between elections when we need to stay
the most active — building relationships, empowering communities, and
ensuring that the voices of the people remain at the center of the
conversation.

But the truth is, organizing year-round comes with real operational costs.
Just take a look at the events we hosted just this week:

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250607.hNmInh&refcode=ema20250607-240140&t=1001&akid=240140%2E444211%2EXI4TIg ]Screenshot of three upcoming Powered by People events. The first event
is 'Voter Registration Event at the SE Houston Town Hall Meeting.' The
second event is 'Volunteer Deputy Registrar (VDR) Training.' The third
event is 'Powered by People Volunteer Training (Voter Registration Events
and REACH) (2025).'[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250607.hNmInh&refcode=ema20250607-240140&t=1002&akid=240140%2E444211%2EXI4TIg ]Screenshot of an event listing for 'PxP Host
Training – VR Events (VDR & REACH Training Required).'

These programs require funding to keep growing and build momentum.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65203?c0=pbp-em-20250607.VGWxsR&refcode=ema20250607-240140&t=1003&akid=240140%2E444211%2EXI4TIg ]That’s where you come in. Right now, we’re asking
if you can help continue our vital work and strengthen these efforts by
chipping in $20.35 today—our average donation for the month of May.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65203?c0=pbp-em-20250607.VGWxsR&refcode=ema20250607-240140&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=240140%2E444211%2EXI4TIg ]DONATE $20.35

Every dollar you contribute will help us organize, mobilize, and stay
connected to the communities we serve while pushing for progress — no
matter the election cycle.

Thank you,

Powered by People

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1005&akid=240140%2E444211%2EXI4TIg

* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**First trial of immigrant accused of trespassing on Texas border military zone ends in acquittal,** " Texas Tribune's**Uriel J. Garcia** - "A federal jury and judge acquitted a 21-year-old woman from Peru on Thursday of illegally entering a national defense area and military reservation after she crossed the Rio Grande from Mexico into Texas last month, in what lawyers say is the first trial of an immigrant since the Trump administration declared parts of the New Mexico and Texas border as a military zone.

**Adely Vanessa De La Cruz-Alvarez** was detained by soldiers and arrested by a Border Patrol agent on May 12 after they found her alone sitting next to the gate on a bollard wall near Tornillo, 40 miles east of El Paso.

"Hopefully, this sets the tone for the federal government," said **Veronica Teresa Lerma** , one of her defense attorneys, adding, "so they know what the El Paso community will do with these charges."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/f2eb29be-8f0f-4669-8ebc-f362486566c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Earlier this year, the Trump administration designated 180 miles of the New Mexico border with Mexico and 63 miles in West Texas as a national defense area, overseen by U.S. Army commands based out of Fort Huachuca in Arizona and Fort Bliss in El Paso.

At the start of the trial, De La Cruz-Alvarez, who has been in immigration agents' custody since her arrest, faced three misdemeanor charges: entering the country illegally, violating national defense property and trespassing onto a military base. She was found guilty of entering the country illegally and was already facing deportation as soon as she was arrested.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/f2eb29be-8f0f-4669-8ebc-f362486566c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
She was sentenced to time served plus one business day by U.S. Magistrate Judge **Laura Enriquez**. Even though she was acquitted of trespassing onto a military zone, De La Cruz-Alvarez will most likely get deported, said **Shane Michael McMahon** , her public defense attorney.

"She knew exactly what she was doing," **Phillip Douglas Countryman** , the federal prosecutor, told the judge, asking for a 45-day jail sentence. Crossing the border illegally "should come with some sort of punishment."

The verdict is the latest blow to the Trump administration, which pursued additional misdemeanor charges in addition to the usual misdemeanor charge of entering the U.S. improperly or the felony charge of reentering the country after being denied. Tacking on the trespassing charges, is the latest tactic the administration is trying in its attempt to deport and punish 1 million immigrants annually, including some who have entered the country legally." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/f138a44f-a178-4aea-aee8-7e172ba0535f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* **#TXLEGE**

"**Speaker Dustin Burrows, once tagged as "liberal," kept skeptics at bay by leaning into a conservative agenda,**" Texas Tribune's**Kayla Guo** - "Back in December, Rep. **Dustin Burrows** ' grasp on the speakership was, at best, tenuous.

His main rival, Rep. **David Cook** of Mansfield, had already won the endorsement of the House Republican Caucus, a victory his backers argued should've clinched his ascent to speaker of the GOP-controlled Texas House.

Instead, Burrows, surrounded by Republicans willing to defy caucus rules, [claimed just minutes later](https://substack.com/redirect/e5532239-9c0c-4b34-aea5-13b6d4933635?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the race was over and that he had the 76 votes needed to lock up the gavel. He touted an even mix of Democratic and GOP support, though some Republicans immediately asked for their names to be removed, pushing him back under the threshold to win.

The competing pronouncements deepened a bitter, months-long power struggle within a Republican Party that had churned through three speakers in four sessions, and it amplified demands by grassroots activists to sideline Democrats and lean into the party's most partisan impulses.

Cook carried the banner of the hardline, so-called "reformers," whose [allies](https://substack.com/redirect/36aa4df7-f391-47dc-a552-29b2cf6c40c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [outside](https://substack.com/redirect/58225528-bad8-4096-98e0-8453dadb524f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [the chamber](https://substack.com/redirect/7ed2a83a-b382-46ef-a55c-8e74f78ddc8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) maligned Burrows as a "liberal" intent on empowering Democrats. Burrows, in fact a staunch conservative and a key player in the inner circle of House leadership, represented a continuation of the establishment that the party's rightmost faction had spent years fighting to depose.

Burrows [won the race](https://substack.com/redirect/fc56d07f-6884-45c8-8550-e7873cb45674?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), finally, on the first day of the legislative session, with a coalition made up of 49 Democrats and 36 Republicans. His reliance on the minority party prompted [immediate attacks](https://substack.com/redirect/c529ebdb-3fa6-418e-8e79-fb67cbbe71c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from his right flank and fueled charges that he would cater to the Democratic bloc that drove his victory. Some of the newly seated "reformers" openly threatened retribution in next year's primaries for colleagues who had backed him.

The speaker's race was over, but now Burrows would have to govern a House wracked by months of acrimony, raising questions about how much could get done with one chamber of the Legislature at war with itself.

Instead, the Lubbock Republican emerged 140 days later from his first session wielding the gavel with a laundry list of new conservative laws to point to, having almost entirely avoided the turmoil his election portended.

"With 150 members, you're always going to have 150 perspectives -- that's part of what makes our chamber unique," Burrows said in a statement. "But the speaker's job isn't to force uniformity or go to battle with the Senate on every topic, it's to protect the institution by making sure members have the tools and support they need to succeed on behalf of their districts and go home to show real accomplishments to the people they represent. I think we accomplished that."

Burrows began the session with a clear eye toward retaining the speakership and detaching himself from the tenure of his now-politically radioactive predecessor, Beaumont Rep. **Dade Phelan**. He aligned the House closely with Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** , the powerful presiding officer of the Senate and frequent antagonist of past speakers, and built goodwill with Gov. **Greg Abbott** by prioritizing school vouchers, the governor's top priority. And he worked methodically to win over skeptical members of his caucus, giving some committee chairmanships and making sure others saw their pet issues reach the floor.

A careful tactician with years of experience in House leadership, Burrows managed to deliver almost all major Republican priorities, including vouchers, tougher bail laws and a raft of socially conservative policies. Yet he did so without fully alienating the Democrats who powered him to the speaker's chair, even as they saw their party's priorities die and failed to stop most of the GOP's top items.

"We really didn't know what to expect because of how he was elected," said Rep. **Mitch Little,** a Lewisville Republican and Cook devotee who [once slammed](https://substack.com/redirect/e5532239-9c0c-4b34-aea5-13b6d4933635?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Burrows' supporters for making a "brazen attempt to circumvent the will of the voters."

"But to be quite honest," Little said at the end of the session, "if he had been elected by all Republicans, I don't know how this session would have really gone any differently." Complaining about the few unfinished items, he added, would be "picking nits more than anything."" [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/7059f8ac-3c2f-4d67-a865-adc20992feeb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* **STATE GOVERNMENT**

"**Legislators give Texas Space Commission $300M for grants and the power to close highways and beaches,** " Houston Chronicle's**Andrea Leinfelder** - "Texas legislators approved $300 million in grant money [for the Texas Space Commission](https://substack.com/redirect/9919a9eb-5988-4f5f-a55d-fff51ca238f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and gave it new authority to close highways and beaches "to promote space-related industries" during the final days of the state's 89th Legislative Session.

The ability to close public areas was a last-minute addition [after a different bill](https://substack.com/redirect/01bf8be2-6c54-4d2b-9a98-06e92343876a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) languished in the House.

"The future is being shaped right here in Texas," state Rep. **Greg Bonnen** , R-Friendswood, [said Sunday when trying to pass the bill](https://substack.com/redirect/016299b7-8fdd-473e-b6b8-00fee9dabf46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). "What is happening in our state is literally equivalent to what happened at Kitty Hawk in 1903 when the Wright brothers first flew."

Bonnen led the legislation that created the Texas Space Commission during the 2023 legislative session, and he authored [House Bill 5246](https://substack.com/redirect/09fa3ffe-5b15-496c-8a96-8fadb6520eea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) this session to clarify the commission's powers and duties. The bill still needs to be signed by Gov.**Greg Abbot** t.

This legislation covers a wide variety of topics, but the ability to close highways and beaches caught legislators' attention. HB 5246 gives the commission's board the power to close highways, venues and beaches - with approval of the city where these are located - to keep the public safe during rocket launches.

This most directly relates to**Elon Musk 's** SpaceX, which needs to close a public highway and beach when it launches its Starship rocket in South Texas. Cameron County has been in charge of closing the road and beach, and starting Sept. 1 the Space Commission will work with the newly created city of Starbase - not the county - to shut down access.

"Who is in the best position to have the public interest in mind in closing a public beach?" said state Rep. **Rafael Anch ia**, D-Dallas. "It's not the people in the company town that is effectively a wholly owned subsidiary of SpaceX. And it's not the Space Commission. It is the elected officials who are closest to the people, and that is county government."

Cameron County Judge **Eddie Trevi ño Jr**. [told the Brownsville Herald](https://substack.com/redirect/3bd10b91-b1cd-466e-9ef1-b1224bd4ff2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that he was disappointed with the change, which he described as widely unpopular. He wants the Space Commission and Starbase to respect the public's environmental concerns and beach access - though the county has received its share of criticism regarding these topics.

"I think this is raising a lot of questions and concerns with regards to the future authority and regulation of the closures," Treviño said. "As county judge I'm responsible for the people. A lot of people are upset."

Starbase did not immediately respond to a request for comment. A spokesperson for the Texas Space Commission said it's reviewing the legislation "to be prepared when those new laws take effect."" [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/990e3f74-a4da-4fb8-b3dc-20fb7ded37ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **2025**

"**Early votes up, but voter turnout for June 7 mayoral runoff 'still abysmal',**" San Antonio Express-News' **Molly Smith** - "After the field of 27 mayoral candidates was cut to two last month, San Antonio voters have gotten more excited about selecting the city's next leader.

More than 98,000 city voters cast in-person ballots during the eight-day early voting period, which ended Tuesday. That's up 33% from the nearly [74,000 Bexar County voters](https://substack.com/redirect/fcf4a632-00cc-4bee-8d42-e1a5a390eb6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who voted early for the May 3 election, which featured municipal races in San Antonio, Balcones Heights, Olmos Park, Kirby and Von Ormy, in addition to a slew of school board elections and an Alamo Community College District bond.

Only five San Antonio city races are on the [June 7 runoff ballot](https://substack.com/redirect/5cff780f-4ce7-434e-831a-8246268888e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): the open mayor's seat and four City Council seats.

**Gina Ortiz Jones** , a one-time Air Force undersecretary, and **Rolando Pablos** , a former Texas secretary of state, are battling to succeed term-limited Mayor **Ron Nirenberg**.

The in-person votes and the nearly 4,000 mailed-in ballots exceed the 101,150 San Antonians who voted in the May election, both early and on election day.

One possible reason?

"Now that it's whittled down to two, you don't have to do as much work to understand who they are and what they stand for," said campaign consultant **Laura Barberena**. "You're asking a little bit less of the voter and I think that they're more receptive to participating in a place where they don't feel like they're making an uninformed vote."

Barberena managed Councilman **Manny Pelaez 's** unsuccessful mayoral campaign (he came in fifth place) and is currently managing**Ivalis Meza Gonzalez 's** campaign for Pelaez's open District 8 seat on the Northwest Side.

Campaign spending could be another factor behind the bigger early voting totals.

"A lot of money has been put on the ground to raise peoples' awareness that this election is going on," Barberena said.

Political action committees, many of which have a partisan bent, [have spent more than $1 million](https://substack.com/redirect/8c23f45e-1a4e-4a5e-8c47-88a52024f0b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in recent weeks trying to get their preferred candidate elected by blanketing voters with mailers and television and digital ads." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/f777879c-139b-4365-8085-86c233792c09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

"**Jolanda Jones jumps into race for Houston's 18th Congressional District,** " Houston Chronicle's **Taylor Goldenstein** - "Houston Democratic state Rep. **Jolanda Jones** is throwing her name in the hat for a run in Texas' 18th Congressional District, formerly held by the late **Sylvester Turner**.

Jones, who has represented the south and southeast Houston in the Texas House since 2022, was waiting until the end of the legislative session on Monday to make a decision about whether to run.

"After much encouragement and serious consideration, I have decided today to become a candidate for Congress in the historic 18th district," Jones said in a statement Thursday.

Jones joins an increasingly crowded field that includes Harris County Attorney **Christian Menefee** , former Houston City Council member **Amanda Edwards** and a former senior advisor to the late U.S. Rep. **Sheila Jackson Lee** , **Isaiah Martin**. The district leans heavily Democratic and has a long history of being represented by Black legislators.

In 2023, as a freshman, Jones made headlines [after her entire House staff resigned](https://substack.com/redirect/4f10ada1-3b2c-49e9-b58f-964a6b6e042c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), alleging she created an "abusive and hostile" work environment. They claimed in a leaked resignation that Jones hid an inappropriate relationship between her son and an intern and allowed him outsized say in state business.

Jones never addressed the allegations directly, saying she could not discuss personnel matters.

In this latest legislative session, she authored successful bills [ensuring low-level criminals are not held in jail pretrial](https://substack.com/redirect/50b0c2d6-fdc9-4a2e-aa3e-38fce86d98c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for longer than the maximum term of confinement for their alleged crime and [requiring high school students to learn about civics](https://substack.com/redirect/850a9419-6c70-48a7-a50e-a592b0bcd74c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as part of their social studies curriculum.

Jones previously served on the Houston City Council from 2008 to 2012 and Houston ISD's school board from 2016 until 2020. The criminal defense attorney also owns her own firm." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/329e909f-5bc9-4b65-90ef-7f0d666163b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **2026**

"**Can an astronaut, pastor or linebacker help Democrats finally win in Texas?** " Houston Chronicle's **Jeremy Wallace** - "A pastor, an astronaut and a linebacker walk into a bar.

Actually, it's not a bar, but the potential Democratic primary looming in 2026 for **John Cornyn's** U.S. Senate seat.

And don't count out the former punk rock guitarist who might just have another run in him.

With Austin state Rep. **James Talarico** , a 36-year-old former teacher and pastor, [telling](https://substack.com/redirect/b29ea687-cc27-4d28-8d60-7437d6750618?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Edward McKinley** this week that he is thinking about getting into the race, that colorful primary field is starting to look more plausible.

"The legislative session just ended, and I am having conversations about how I can best serve, and that does include the Senate race," Talarico said.

In Houston,**Terry Virts** , a former astronaut and retired fighter pilot, has been building up potential staff and using social media to give a flavor of the type of campaign he might run on if he gets in.

"Our draft-dodger in chief is throwing himself a $50M birthday party with Tanks, APCs, Missiles, and 6,600 soldiers -- all while cutting kids' cancer research," [Virts, 57, wrote in one post](https://substack.com/redirect/9861f292-7cb9-44f4-9e5a-81e53e89ae10?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) last week blasting President **Donald Trump**.

The linebacker, of course, is **Colin Allred** , the 42-year-old former Dallas Congressman who ran against U.S. **Ted Cruz** in 2024. He has [already said he is considering running for the seat](https://substack.com/redirect/84b2b9b2-0da4-461c-9565-615ca49ffa56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which Cornyn has held since 2002.

Meanwhile **Beto O 'Rourke**, the former punk rock guitarist and El Paso congressman, was just in Humble for [a rally that drew 1,500 people](https://substack.com/redirect/48d3b1be-a020-4b72-8bb6-c7b4c7be9940?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), making clear he hasn't ruled out taking another shot at higher office.

"The bottom line for me is I'm gonna do whatever I can that is most helpful and most useful -- if that's running, if it's not running. I have no pride in this," the 52-year-old said." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/a59a42fc-c7e7-4737-8af0-dfaa5a6aa048?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **BUSINESS NEWS**

"**New Linde facility in Freeport expected to double liquefied carbon dioxide production,** " Houston Chronicle's **Yvette Orosco** - "Linde, [an industrial gases and engineering company](https://substack.com/redirect/6c5ff479-18f2-4f17-aa82-fa99a3ea0572?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), will add a second carbon dioxide-producing facility at its Freeport site, according to a [company announcement](https://substack.com/redirect/c298a780-fa23-47a4-8ba5-a278a3e3e435?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Once completed in 2027, the facility would liquefy carbon dioxide collected at MEGlobal American's Oyster Creek ethylene glycol facility, which is supplied with oxygen by Linde's air separation plant.

Linde says the new facility would feed growing demand for carbon dioxide production.

"Our investment further strengthens Linde's robust CO2 network, enabling reliable supply to customers throughout the region while positioning us for future growth," Linde CEO **Amer Akhras** said in the company's news release.

Linde said capturing and reusing carbon dioxide from industrial production prevents it from being emitted into the atmosphere.

Carbon dioxide is used to create the fizz in carbonated beverages. It can also be used in food preservation and in making dry ice.

Carbon dioxide is a colorless, odorless gas that naturally forms in the Earth's atmosphere, according to [U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. ](https://substack.com/redirect/300b9c8b-34d2-4015-b8cf-33205913e9fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

CO2 emissions, however, are recognized as pollutants that contribute to climate change, are regulated by the EPA under the Clean Air Act.

Environmental advocacy groups such as the [Natural Resources Defense Council](https://substack.com/redirect/98d3b85d-42b8-4213-8f42-8030c2cc8757?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Environmental Health Project](https://substack.com/redirect/66a8b2af-58ed-457d-be2d-78656d75f134?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), have cited a study in 2024 [by the U.S. Department of Energy](https://substack.com/redirect/f33b7730-ccd8-4d75-81a3-29c26f9a607f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that details potential environmental threats created by liquefication plants, including carbon and methane emissions and the possible effects on air and water quality." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/afd6303c-e7fd-4f54-911e-ae9b473198f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

* **TRUMP ADMINISTRATION**

"**Young UTSA grad 's first big job? He's head of federal counterterrorism office,**" San Antonio Express-News' **Sig Christenson** and**Scott Huddleston** - "At 22,**Thomas Fugate III** is going places fast in the Trump administration.

A MAGA loyalist, he's risen from working on**Donald Trump 's** 2024 presidential campaign to heading up, apparently on an interim basis, a counterterrorism office in the Department of Homeland Security -- a job that in the past required the boss to have a thicker resume.

Fugate, who graduated magna cum laude from the University of Texas at San Antonio in 2024, has no previous experience combating terrorism.

DHS defended the decision to hire Fugate, saying he's a self-starter who has impressed the department's higher-ups.

"Tom Fugate has performed well in his current role as a confidential assistant in our Immigration & Border Security suboffice," a DHS official told the San Antonio Express-News. "Due to his success, he has been temporarily given additional leadership responsibilities in the Center for Prevention Programs and Partnerships office (CP3). This is a credit to his work ethic and success on the job."

**Bill Braniff** , the director Fugate replaced, is an Army veteran who'd previously served as [director of the University of Maryland's ](https://substack.com/redirect/0ef62c50-25fe-4f70-8161-b6948f64b6a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)National Consortium for the Study of Terrorism and Responses to Terrorism. He is currently the executive director of American University's Polarization and Extremism Research and Innovation Lab.

He stepped down as CP3 director on March 3 as the Trump administration began cutting his staff.

"Over the course of the weekend prior, eight dedicated and effective public servants were wrongfully terminated via email due to their probationary status," Braniff said in a statement posted on his LinkedIn page. "As my term appointment was ending, the best thing I could do for them and for CP3 was to resign alongside of them, as some agencies and departments have rehired people in mission critical offices once they were made aware of the implications of those terminations."

ProPublica, a nonprofit news organization that broke the news of Fugate's appointment, reported that CP3 staffers expressed shock at how little Fugate knows about the basics of his role and likened meetings with him to "career counseling."

DHS did not address questions from the Express-News about his experience." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/af249748-0b39-49cc-aa30-ccc5730d97f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
